reviews.review_description: I like me a good arcade shooter, if it looks good and feels right. This one is very half-baked in comparison to Dead Second and Crisis Brigade 2. You're on a rail with no choice of where to move next. Kill everyone in the area, and the game moves you forward for you (which feels kinda jarring). There are several guns to choose from but they all feel the same. The graphics are the worst part. Strangely, they don't look anywhere near as good as the preview video (which doesn't actually say "actual footage from Quest 3" so maybe they were from a PC). Objects are very blocky simple colored polygons with no shadows, so it's hard to tell what is what. Maybe that's why all enemies are surrounded by a thick, bright-red halo (so you can figure out what you're supposed to fire at). At worst, objects in the near distance fade in as you approach - you can't see anything far away. There are much better options to spend your time playing.

I seen this game pop up on side quest and the trailer looked pretty fun, I always liked that style shooter like Gladiatus. The price was little high but I thought If I didn't like it I could always refund in first 30 min.  Well I played for last couple hours and it's really fun and has few areas love to see improve. It's sorta free movement, you have a big green circle you can move around in and once you complete area it travels you forward.  The gun play is actually pretty nice I do wish that manual reload would be an option for future. There are several maps including zombie level and minigames.  There is a lot to unlock and that is were the replay ability comes in plus there is 3 different difficulties.  One big issue I had over and over was if you bend down to dodge bullets or to get behind something when you get up you are way to tall and have to reset hight in pause menu everytime that was biggest thing that needs fixed also manual reload be nice besides that pretty good game that I could see on Meta Store really soon. Update I have changed score from a 4 to 5 because this game is addicting trying unlock stuff and the levels are so beautifully made in this art style.  I can't believe I haven't seen any VR YouTubers review yet, but I'm leaving messages on all there channels to try it.  If developer keeps updating with new levels and possibly multiplayer PvP which would be cool if you moved like it currently does but the player your going against is moving like that but on opposite sides and building and other props were in between both of you that could be  different enough to make this game be a hit stand out from other shooters.  KEEP UP GREAT WORK!!!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the positive feedback. We really enjoyed making this game and from your post, it seems we hit the old-school arcade feel as intended. 

You are able to manually reload with X/A Buttons! We will be looking into the height issue and try to replicate it on our end.

We've discussed Multiplayer quite a lot and hope the game does well enough to support it.

reviews.review_description: I was able to be lucky enough to have an early access version of HeadCount and i instantly fell in love with the game. I love everything about it, the artwork, style, everything. I quickly picked up on all the controls and how the game worked. You can move around in a green circle and once you have completed the section you are in it moves it on. The cards system on how you collect guns is really nice and definitley kept me playing to collect all of the guns. This is definitley a great VR game for everyone and worth every penny. Keep up the great work guys and I hope to see a multiplayer version soon!
Nice game but i dont like the aiming device on the guns and there is no option to lose them as far as i can see, Quick aiming is to hard this way

Very good game! It's just fun to scurry through the levels on skis, knocking down enemies and dodging bullets. Absolutely sufficient at the moment for the reduced price. More content later would be great and a co-op mode would be an absolute dream!
